contents.value:
```php
echo(string ...$expressions): void
```
Outputs one or more expressions. `echo` is not a function but a language construct, so parentheses are not required. It has no return value.

```php
print(string $expression): int
```
Outputs a string. `print` is a language construct that accepts a single argument and always returns `1`. Slightly slower than `echo`.

```php
var_dump(mixed $value, mixed ...$values): void
```
Displays structured information about one or more expressions including its type and value. Arrays and objects are explored recursively with values indented to show structure.

```php
print_r(mixed $value, bool $return = false): string|bool
```
Prints human-readable information about a variable. If `$return` is `true`, returns the output as a string instead of printing it.

```php
var_export(mixed $value, bool $return = false): ?string
```
Outputs or returns a parsable string representation of a variable. Differs from `var_dump()` in that the returned representation is valid PHP code.

```php
printf(string $format, mixed ...$values): int
```
Outputs a formatted string. The format string may contain conversion specifications starting with `%` (e.g. `%s`, `%d`, `%f`). Returns the length of the outputted string.

```php
sprintf(string $format, mixed ...$values): string
```
Returns a string produced according to the formatting string `$format`. Uses the same format specifiers as `printf()`.

```php
strlen(string $string): int
```
Returns the length of the given string in bytes. For multi-byte characters, use `mb_strlen()` instead.

```php
strpos(string $haystack, string $needle, int $offset = 0): int|false
```
Finds the numeric position of the first occurrence of `$needle` in `$haystack`. Returns `false` if not found. Use `!== false` for comparison.

```php
strrpos(string $haystack, string $needle, int $offset = 0): int|false
```
Finds the numeric position of the last occurrence of `$needle` in `$haystack`. Returns `false` if not found.

```php
substr(string $string, int $offset, ?int $length = null): string
```
Returns the portion of `$string` specified by the `$offset` and `$length` parameters. Negative offset counts from the end of the string.

```php
str_replace(
    array|string $search,
    array|string $replace,
    string|array $subject,
    int &$count = null
): string|array
```
Replaces all occurrences of `$search` with `$replace` in `$subject`. If `$search` and `$replace` are arrays, a mapping is applied.

```php
str_contains(string $haystack, string $needle): bool
```
(PHP 8.0+) Performs a case-sensitive check to determine if `$haystack` contains `$needle`. Returns `true` if found, `false` otherwise.

```php
str_starts_with(string $haystack, string $needle): bool
```
(PHP 8.0+) Performs a case-sensitive check to determine if `$haystack` begins with `$needle`.

```php
str_ends_with(string $haystack, string $needle): bool
```
(PHP 8.0+) Performs a case-sensitive check to determine if `$haystack` ends with `$needle`.

```php
strtolower(string $string): string
```
Returns a string with all ASCII alphabetic characters converted to lowercase. For Unicode, use `mb_strtolower()`.

```php
strtoupper(string $string): string
```
Returns a string with all ASCII alphabetic characters converted to uppercase. For Unicode, use `mb_strtoupper()`.

```php
trim(string $string, string $characters = " \n\r\t\v\0"): string
```
Strips whitespace (or other characters) from the beginning and end of a string. Without the second parameter, trims common whitespace characters.

```php
ltrim(string $string, string $characters = " \n\r\t\v\0"): string
```
Strips whitespace (or other characters) from the beginning of a string.

```php
rtrim(string $string, string $characters = " \n\r\t\v\0"): string
```
Strips whitespace (or other characters) from the end of a string. `chop()` is an alias of this function.

```php
explode(string $separator, string $string, int $limit = PHP_INT_MAX): array
```
Returns an array of strings split by `$separator`. If `$limit` is set, the returned array will contain a maximum of `$limit` elements.

```php
implode(string $separator, array $array): string
```
Joins array elements with the `$separator` string and returns the resulting string. `join()` is an alias.

```php
htmlspecialchars(
    string $string,
    int $flags = ENT_QUOTES | ENT_SUBSTITUTE,
    ?string $encoding = null,
    bool $double_encode = true
): string
```
Converts special characters (`&`, `"`, `'`, `<`, `>`) to HTML entities. Essential for preventing XSS attacks when outputting user data.

```php
urlencode(string $string): string
```
Returns a string in which all non-alphanumeric characters except `-_.` have been replaced with a percent sign followed by two hex digits, and spaces are encoded as `+`.

```php
base64_encode(string $string): string
```
Encodes the given data with Base64. This encoding is designed to make binary data survive transport through non-binary-safe channels.

```php
base64_decode(string $string, bool $strict = false): string|false
```
Decodes a Base64-encoded string. If `$strict` is `true`, returns `false` if input contains characters outside the Base64 alphabet.

```php
md5(string $string, bool $binary = false): string
```
Calculates the MD5 hash of a string. Returns a 32-character hexadecimal number. **Not suitable for cryptographic purposes** — use `password_hash()` for passwords.

```php
number_format(
    float $num,
    int $decimals = 0,
    ?string $decimal_separator = '.',
    ?string $thousands_separator = ','
): string
```
Formats a number with grouped thousands and optional decimal places.

```php
array_push(array &$array, mixed ...$values): int
```
Pushes one or more elements onto the end of an array. Returns the new number of elements. Equivalent to `$array[] = $value` for a single value.

```php
array_pop(array &$array): mixed
```
Pops and returns the last element of the array, shortening it by one element. Returns `null` if the array is empty.

```php
array_shift(array &$array): mixed
```
Shifts the first value of the array off and returns it, shortening the array by one element and re-indexing numeric keys.

```php
array_unshift(array &$array, mixed ...$values): int
```
Prepends one or more elements to the beginning of an array. Returns the new number of elements. All numerical keys are re-indexed starting from zero.

```php
array_merge(array ...$arrays): array
```
Merges one or more arrays. Values with numeric keys are appended and re-indexed; values with string keys are overwritten by later arrays.

```php
array_map(?callable $callback, array $array, array ...$arrays): array
```
Applies the `$callback` to the elements of the given arrays and returns a new array containing the results. If `$callback` is `null`, creates an array of arrays.

```php
array_filter(array $array, ?callable $callback = null, int $mode = 0): array
```
Filters elements of an array using a callback function. If no callback is supplied, all falsy entries are removed. Keys are preserved.

```php
array_reduce(array $array, callable $callback, mixed $initial = null): mixed
```
Iteratively reduces the array to a single value using a callback. The callback receives `($carry, $item)` where `$carry` is the return value of the previous iteration.

```php
array_keys(array $array, mixed $filter_value = null, bool $strict = false): array
```
Returns all the keys or a subset of the keys of an array. If `$filter_value` is specified, only keys containing that value are returned.

```php
array_values(array $array): array
```
Returns all the values from the array and indexes the array numerically. Useful for re-indexing after `array_filter()`.

```php
array_unique(array $array, int $flags = SORT_STRING): array
```
Removes duplicate values from an array. The first occurrence of each value is kept and keys are preserved.

```php
array_key_exists(string|int $key, array $array): bool
```
Returns `true` if the given key/index exists in the array. Unlike `isset()`, returns `true` even if the value is `null`.

```php
in_array(mixed $needle, array $haystack, bool $strict = false): bool
```
Searches for `$needle` in `$haystack`. If `$strict` is `true`, uses identity comparison (`===`). Returns `true` if found.

```php
count(Countable|array $value, int $mode = COUNT_NORMAL): int
```
Counts all elements in an array or a `Countable` object. If `$mode` is `COUNT_RECURSIVE`, counts elements of multidimensional arrays recursively.

```php
sort(array &$array, int $flags = SORT_REGULAR): true
```
Sorts an array in ascending order by value. Existing keys are discarded and the array is re-indexed. Modifies the array in place.

```php
usort(array &$array, callable $callback): true
```
Sorts an array by values using a user-defined comparison function. The callback must return an integer less than, equal to, or greater than zero.

```php
array_search(mixed $needle, array $haystack, bool $strict = false): int|string|false
```
Searches the array for `$needle` and returns the corresponding key if found, `false` otherwise. Use `$strict = true` for type-safe comparison.

```php
array_column(array $array, int|string|null $column_key, int|string|null $index_key = null): array
```
Returns the values from a single column of the input array. Optionally, an `$index_key` may be provided to index the returned array by that column.

```php
array_chunk(array $array, int $length, bool $preserve_keys = false): array
```
Splits an array into chunks of `$length` size. The last chunk may contain fewer elements.

```php
compact(array|string $var_name, array|string ...$var_names): array
```
Creates an array containing variables and their values. The variable names are passed as strings.

```php
range(string|int|float $start, string|int|float $end, int|float $step = 1): array
```
Creates an array containing a range of elements. Works with integers, floats, and single characters.

```php
json_encode(mixed $value, int $flags = 0, int $depth = 512): string|false
```
Returns the JSON representation of a value. Common flags: `JSON_PRETTY_PRINT`, `JSON_UNESCAPED_UNICODE`, `JSON_THROW_ON_ERROR`.

```php
json_decode(
    string $json,
    ?bool $associative = null,
    int $depth = 512,
    int $flags = 0
): mixed
```
Decodes a JSON string. When `$associative` is `true`, JSON objects are returned as associative arrays instead of `stdClass` objects.

```php
file_get_contents(
    string $filename,
    bool $use_include_path = false,
    ?resource $context = null,
    int $offset = 0,
    ?int $length = null
): string|false
```
Reads entire file into a string. Preferred way to read file contents. Also supports HTTP/HTTPS URLs when `allow_url_fopen` is enabled.

```php
file_put_contents(
    string $filename,
    mixed $data,
    int $flags = 0,
    ?resource $context = null
): int|false
```
Writes data to a file. Equivalent to calling `fopen()`, `fwrite()`, and `fclose()`. Use `FILE_APPEND` flag to append instead of overwrite.

```php
isset(mixed $var, mixed ...$vars): bool
```
Determines if a variable is declared and is different than `null`. If multiple parameters are supplied, returns `true` only if all of them are set. Does not generate a warning if the variable doesn't exist.

```php
empty(mixed $var): bool
```
Determines whether a variable is considered empty. The following are empty: `""`, `0`, `0.0`, `"0"`, `null`, `false`, `[]`, and unset variables.

```php
header(string $header, bool $replace = true, int $response_code = 0): void
```
Sends a raw HTTP header. Must be called before any actual output is sent. Use `$replace = false` to send multiple headers of the same type.

```php
die(string|int $message = ""): never
```
Equivalent to `exit()`. Outputs a message and terminates the current script. If `$message` is an integer, it will be used as the exit status.

```php
date(string $format, ?int $timestamp = null): string
```
Returns a string formatted according to the given format using the given timestamp or the current time if no timestamp is given. Common format chars: `Y` (year), `m` (month), `d` (day), `H` (hour), `i` (minutes), `s` (seconds).

```php
strtotime(string $datetime, ?int $baseTimestamp = null): int|false
```
Parses an English textual datetime description into a Unix timestamp. Supports relative formats like `'+1 day'`, `'next Monday'`, `'last month'`.
